title: Ranunculus polyandrus
intro: Ranunculus polyandrus, also known as buttercup, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive cup-shaped appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus polyandrus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Ranunculus polyandrus is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and in the family Ranunculaceae. This classification places it within the order Ranunculales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further, it is classified as a genus and species within the family Ranunculaceae. Ranunculus polyandrus is also commonly referred to as the Buttercup or Grassleaf Buttercup. It is a perennial flowering plant native to North America.
